title: India's activities in nuclear fusion (SST-1 conductor sourcing and ITER contributions)
authors_or_org: Nuclear Engineering International (trade press)
claim_supported: SST-1's superconducting magnet conductor was originally imported from Japan in the late 1990s but is now available from domestic Indian sources, part of IPR's broader shift toward domestically sourced components for future systems/upgrades; India's ITER contributions include the cryostat (Larsen & Toubro-built, 3,850-tonne, 30m-tall stainless-steel vessel, installed 2020) and cryogenic distribution/cryolines systems, plus diagnostics, power supplies, gyrotron sources, and optical components via ITER-India.
